key_insight: EvoThink reduces LRM overthinking by pruning redundant verification steps and teaching models to prefer “aha-moment” reasoning paths that lead from wrong attempts to correct solutions.
engineering_relevance: The method offers a practical way to lower inference token cost while improving reasoning quality, which is useful for deploying more efficient math and code reasoning systems.
method: Self-Pruning Training (SPT): iteratively removes redundant reasoning steps and self-trains on the shortened trajectories.
Aha-Moment Preference Optimization (AMPO): mines useful failed attempts, synthesizes from-wrong-to-right examples, and optimizes the model to internalize the transition pattern.
Combines efficiency gains with exploration of new reasoning paths instead of only compressing trajectories.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports extensive evaluation on mathematical reasoning and code generation benchmarks, showing reduced inference-time token usage and improved reasoning capability.
watch_item: Because pruning and preference optimization alter reasoning traces, careful tuning is needed to avoid removing genuinely useful verification steps or overfitting to synthesized trajectories.
paper_title: EvoThink: Evolving Thinking in Large Reasoning Models via Self-Pruning and Aha-Moment Preference Optimization
